operator_scope.semantic_signature: scatter overwrite with indexed destination writes, bounds checks, and duplicate-index policy

valid_when: The task is KBX scatter or another indexed write-side operation with overwrite semantics.
invalid_when: The task explicitly asks for scatter_add/index_add additive accumulation.
preconditions: Read dim, index dtype, source shape, duplicate-index policy, and bounds behavior from OpSpec.

mechanism: Scatter overwrite writes source values to indexed destination positions. It is not scatter-add: duplicate indices must follow the task/PyTorch boundary policy rather than accumulating all contributions.

instructions: Validate index dtype and bounds for every destination write.
Implement overwrite semantics for KBX scatter; do not replace it with scatter-add or index_add unless OpSpec says additive accumulation is required.
When duplicate indices may occur, follow the task's nondeterminism/tolerance policy; do not assume unique indices unless OpSpec guarantees them.
Keep source/index/src dimensional constraints explicit and test broadcast prohibition or allowance according to the task.
action.patch_hint: Compute dst_offset from the logical output coordinates with the indexed dimension replaced by index_value; then write src_value under the task's duplicate-index policy.

expected_effect.correctness: Prevents using additive collision semantics or unique-index assumptions for scatter overwrite tasks.
expected_effect.performance: No stable H20 performance claim; duplicate-index behavior and memory coalescing are workload-dependent.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Separates scatter overwrite repair from scatter-add atomic repair.
expected_effect.risk: Floating overwrite order may be nondeterministic under duplicate indices; validation must match task policy.

validation.promotion_policy: Stable semantic rule; performance and determinism policies require run-specific validation.

known_failures: Assuming unique indices can hide nondeterministic duplicate-index behavior.
Removing bounds checks can produce illegal memory writes.
boundary_tests: zero-size indexed dimension if task allows
